code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test20-JUL-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and significant proteinuria, indicating possible nephropathy. Urine is cloudy, brown, with low specific gravity and marked ketonuria, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Metabolic acidosis is suggested by low CO₂. Findings are consistent with acute or chronic kidney disease, possibly with dehydration or catabolic state. Clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.
